articles.title: Chitradurga heatwave intensifies in Karnataka - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Chitradurga in central Karnataka is experiencing a significant rise in temperatures, with residents struggling against intense heat, hot winds, and strong sunlight that have disrupted everyday life across the district.
Known for its historic forts and rocky terrain, the area is enduring unusually severe weather conditions this season. Locals report that the heat is becoming increasingly hard to tolerate during peak daytime hours.
Many residents avoid venturing outdoors between 10 am and 4 pm due to the extreme temperatures. There have been reports of dehydration, dizziness, and fatigue, particularly affecting students, college-goers, and tourists visiting local forts.
People have been relying heavily on water, buttermilk, and fruit juices to manage the heat, with growing concerns about the worsening conditions and their effects on daily life.
Sheema Kausher, Associate Professor at Government Science College, Chitradurga, told ANI, “the temperature levels this year are notably higher compared to previous years, making outdoor conditions more challenging.”
Ramesh Bhat, Associate Professor of Mathematics at the same institution, added, “people are facing significant discomfort due to the scorching heat, particularly in fort and rocky areas where temperatures feel even more intense.”
He also noted that the lack of rainfall has exacerbated the situation, raising health concerns and compelling residents to remain indoors during the hottest hours.
Locals expressed apprehension that if the current trend persists, the coming days may become increasingly difficult, with rising temperatures expected to further affect daily activities and outdoor movement.
Authorities and residents are urging caution as the region continues to endure ongoing heatwave-like conditions.
